text.title: Event-Sourced Aggregate
text.summary: Order aggregate with _apply dispatch, create factory, from_events reconstruct, and pending events pattern.

rule.statement: State changes happen only through events. The aggregate applies events to itself via _apply(event); it never mutates fields directly in command methods.
rule.rationale: If command methods mutate state directly, replay from events produces different state than the live object, destroying the event-sourcing guarantee.

rule.statement: New events generated by command methods are added to _pending_events. They are not yet persisted. The repository drains pending events and appends them to the event store.
rule.rationale: Separating generation from persistence allows the repository to apply optimistic concurrency before appending, preventing lost-update bugs.

rule.statement: from_events(order_id, events) reconstructs the aggregate from stored history by replaying each event with is_new=False. is_new=False means the event is not added to _pending_events.
rule.rationale: Distinguishing new events from historical events prevents the repository from re-appending already-persisted events on the next save.

example.note: Command methods call _apply which both mutates state and queues events. Replay calls _apply with is_new=False.
